description: Statute (‘taqqana’) about the conduct of two slaughterhouses, one at the Great Bazaar and one at the Bath of the Mice, issued by a committee of seven individuals elected by the community. The document records the decision to appoint Yefet b. David to be cantor and the supervisor of butchering instead of his father, who had died. Yefet will be responsible for these two markets in Fustat where kosher meat is available, and must send half of the weekly income to Yoshiyyahu Gaon, head of the Yeshiva in Jerusalem, ca. 1015–1025 CE. Goitein dates the document to after 1024; it must date to before May, 1025, when Shelomo b. Yehosef became Gaʾon (for less than a year until Shelomo b. Yehuda took on the leadership role). (Information from Gil, Palestine, vol. 2, 583-585, #319; Goitein, “The Social Services of the Jewish Community as Reflected in the Cairo Geniza Records,” Jewish Social Studies (1964), 10; and Goitein’s index cards) VMR, EMS, MR
description_he: תקנה על ניהול שני בתי מטבחיים: אחד בשוק הגדול ואחד ברחצה של עכברים. ועדת שבעה בחרה ביפת בן דוד כחזן ומפקח על השחיטה. חייב לשלוח מחצית ההכנסה השבועית ליאשיהו גאון ירושלים. כ-1015–1025.
